hirsch-paper.name: Paper sample (Hirsch 2025)
hirsch-paper.description: Mirrors Hirsch et al. (2025)'s Stockholm cat café setup: 27 cats, 12 operating hours, warm-up included. Sample size large enough for the validation score to stabilise above 80.

paper-weekday.name: Paper sample · Weekday daytime
paper-weekday.description: The paper baseline (27 cats, large café, 12 h with warm-up) at a relaxed weekday pace: sparser arrivals, more patient customers, and a lower cat-rest probability.

paper-holiday-rush.name: Paper sample · Holiday rush
paper-holiday-rush.description: The paper baseline under holiday crowding: dense arrivals, shorter patience, one extra staff to cope, and cats that tire more easily.

paper-cat-nap.name: Paper sample · Cat nap mode
paper-cat-nap.description: The paper baseline with most of the 27 cats resting: higher rest probability and longer rest duration, surfacing the interaction-resource bottleneck.

clinic-weekday.name: Clinic · Weekday hours
clinic-weekday.description: A regular clinic day: 8 waiting chairs, 2 clinicians, moderate patient arrivals, running the pure queueing model with no ambient agent.

clinic-flu-season.name: Clinic · Flu season rush
clinic-flu-season.description: Flu-season crowding: dense patient arrivals with extra chairs and clinicians, to stress waiting-room queueing and clinician utilisation.
